element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundation combat move used throughout game
Movie-based special attack; uses Morph meter resource management
Stealth mechanic for avoiding enemies; optional tactical approach
Mobility enhancement; superdash reaches supersonic speeds
High-damage Morph-dependent weapon; reserved for endgame boss fight against Dorian

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Introduced with first special move usage
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource management system; replenishes slowly when depleted, similar to Earthworm Jim ammo system
Reverts to Stanley Ipkiss form when health depletes; visual consequence system

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating mini-bosses (Mrs. Peenman, Irv and Burt Ripley)
Successful navigation of maze-like level sections
typical_tension_triggers: Navigation through convoluted maze-like level design
Morph meter depletion reducing special ability access
Health depletion approaching pajama transformation threshold
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Varied pacing with emphasis on exploration tension; large level designs create navigational challenge rhythm; special move availability cycles tension between empowered and vulnerable states

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Secret ways to use backgrounds to move around levels; discoverable vertical pathways and hidden routes
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Environmental interaction through background elements; non-obvious traversal options that reward exploration

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction in Ipkiss' apartment and high-rent district; basic punch combat and initial Morph power familiarity
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalating complexity through bank, park, and prison sections; increased enemy variety and more convoluted maze navigation; expanded Morph ability toolkit
difficulty_curve.late_game: Prison section and nightclub present highest maze complexity; escalating boss difficulty; final confrontation with Dorian (who also wears the mask) demands mastery of all abilities
notable_spikes: Prison level maze complexity (noted by critics as confusing)
Final boss Dorian fight (mask vs. mask confrontation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game follows story geography: Ipkiss' apartment → high-rent district → outside areas → bank interior → local park → prison interior → ritzy nightclub. Each location introduces film characters as mini-bosses.
Levels are notably oversized with convoluted, confusing layout structure. Critics specifically cited maze areas as problematic. Levels are too large without clear progression signaling.
Environmental elements can be used as hidden traversal routes. Non-obvious ways to move through levels by interacting with background scenery. Encourages exploration and discovery.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multiple levels feature discoverable background shortcuts

notable_level_patterns.description: Major bosses are characters from the film: Mrs. Peenman (angry landlady), Irv and Burt Ripley (careless repairmen), Dorian Tyrell (final boss). Combat encounters reference movie scenes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Distributed throughout; final is Dorian in nightclub

notable_level_patterns.description: Heavy use of character animations borrowed from film, visual gags, and cartoony art style (based on comic book rather than movie aesthetics). Contributes to thematic coherence but not pacing clarity.
